title: tutorial missing
body:
the README file has this blurb:

> Running pods and containers
>
> Follow this tutorial to get started with KPOD.

"tutorial" is a broken link, and I couldn't find a tutorial in here either...

comments.body: Thanks for pointing this out.  @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
@anarcat You should be able to just pull down the repo and start using the commands just like you would with the Docker CLI.

On 2017-12-11 05:54:43, Daniel J Walsh wrote:
> @anarcat You should be able to just pull down the repo and start using the commands just like you would with the Docker CLI.

Oh don't worry, I know. :) I saw your talk at KubeCon and all. I'm the
one who asked about the missing `d` command (`dockerd`). ;)

I don't exactly need the tutorial myself, I just wanted to point out a
broken link...

Thanks!

Yes thanks for that,this repo was originally copied from CRI-O, and that is why we have the broken link.  I assigned a couple of engineers to take a quick look at it. 

Nice meeting you and feel free to try this out, comment and post PR's

On 2017-12-11 15:54:18, Daniel J Walsh wrote:
> Yes thanks for that,this repo was originally copied from CRI-O, and that is why we have the broken link.  I assigned a couple of engineers to take a quick look at it. 
>
> Nice meeting you and feel free to try this out, comment and post PR's

Same here, and yeah - you'll definitly see a few things come out of
this. ;) What's the best way to reach you, by the way, if i have any
more direct questions?

Thanks!

I think the best way is to raise issues. I'm ipbabble on github and I will
most likely be the one working on tutorials. I plan to start this week.

Often my tutorials help find issues/bugs too. So I open and Issue when I
find something. If there is a particular tutorial you want to see then
please tag me.

William

William Henry
Senior Consulting Software Engineer
Red Hat
Colorado, USA.

I'm dwalsh on IRC and in email to redhat.com.  rhatdan on github and twitter, and most other web apps.

#120 provides a short term fix for the bad link (removed it).  We currently don't have a kpod specific tutorial, but have plans to create one early next year.  Keeping this issue open until that is provided.

On 2017-12-12 07:19:33, Tom Sweeney wrote:
> #120 provides a short term fix for the bad link (removed it).  We currently don't have a kpod specific tutorial, but have plans to create one early next year.  Keeping this issue open until that is provided.

great, thanks!

Is there any ‘kpod run’ functionality yet? I just built again today and I’m
not seeing any ‘run’ function.

I am trying to start the tutorial write up and trying out a few commands.

William

On Tue, Dec 12, 2017 at 10:32 AM anarcat <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> On 2017-12-12 07:19:33, Tom Sweeney wrote:

Sent from Gmail Mobile on iPhone
Run should definitely be in there

@ipbabble if you clone it from github then build and install, it should be there.  I think some of that is still a WIP, especially around volumes.  @mheon would be the best judge.  I don't think it's in a kit yet available via dnf/yum, or if it is the functionality may be less polished.
I think some kpod commands are bundled into our CRI-O packages. Those are likely very old and will definitely not have run.

On 2017-12-12 19:20:07, William Henry wrote:
> Is there any ‘kpod run’ functionality yet? I just built again today and I’m
> not seeing any ‘run’ function.

The standalone binary built from source here has "run", but no network
support yet, see #109 for that.

Duh. I'm traveling, rebooted, started a new Terminal but forgot to set PATH
for /usr/local/bin:$PATH. My bad. I need to just uninstall the other
package.

William

William Henry
Senior Consulting Software Engineer
Red Hat
Colorado, USA.
